theme: Remove key people to shatter a network
title: Shatter the Syndicate
seed_problem: optimal-percolation network dismantling under a node-removal budget
mutation: composite: bond-percolation giant-component dismantling summed over S deterministic threshold-coupled scenarios, coupled to a cost-weighted node-immunization budget K (not a plain head count); the population is planted as several dense, internally-redundant communities (plain clusters or near-clique hub cores) stitched by low-degree cut-vertex bridges, so the objective landscape rewards radius-ELL collective influence over raw degree or betweenness.
strategies: trivial: immunize nobody (reproduces the checker baseline B, ratio 0.1)

greedy: immunize people in decreasing raw structural degree, skipping anyone who would blow the budget (the obvious hub-removal recipe; hubs sit inside redundantly connected communities so removing them barely dents the percolating giant, and it never reaches the low-degree bridge people)

strong: adaptive collective influence -- CI_ell(i) = (deg_i-1) * sum of (deg_j-1) over the radius-ell frontier, recomputed after every removal, ranked by CI-per-cost so the budget is spent efficiently; a bridge's frontier reaches a whole neighbouring community giving it outsized CI despite minimal own degree

general: could evaluate exact marginal giant-component reduction per candidate via incremental union-find, use articulation-point/2-edge-connectivity decomposition, or a fractional relaxation of the cost-budget knapsack
